ddls:
CREATE TABLE "user_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier of the user performing the interaction */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Identifier of the node being interacted with */,
  "click_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the click was registered */,
  "selected_multi" INTEGER /* Indicates if multiple selections are allowed */,
  "auto_cancel" INTEGER /* Indicates if auto-cancel behavior is applied */,
  "is_selected" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the node is currently selected */,
  "operation" TEXT /* Describes the operation performed on the selected node */,
  "selection_mode_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the selection mode used during the interaction */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operation performed during the interaction */,
  "interaction_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the interaction */,
  "keyboard_modifiers" TEXT /* Keyboard modifiers used during the interaction (e.g., Ctrl, Meta) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_interactions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_interactions_selection_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("selection_mode_id") REFERENCES selection_modes ("mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_interactions_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "preferences" TEXT /* JSON object containing user preferences for interaction settings */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last activity on the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "selection_modes" (
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the selection mode */,
  "mode_name" TEXT /* Name of the selection mode (e.g., single, multi) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the selection mode */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the selection mode is the default for new users */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the selection mode was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operations" (
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the operation */,
  "operation_name" TEXT /* Name of the operation (e.g., select, deselect) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the operation entails */,
  "is_reversible" INTEGER /* Indicates if the operation can be reversed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the operation was defined */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "click_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each click log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier of the user who clicked */,
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the interaction associated with the click */,
  "click_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the click occurred */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Identifier of the node being clicked */,
  "click_type" TEXT /* Type of click (e.g., single, double) */,
  "click_location" TEXT /* Location on the node where the click occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_click_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_click_logs_interaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("interaction_id") REFERENCES user_interactions ("interaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the preference entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier of the user to whom the preference belongs */,
  "preference_key" TEXT /* Key representing the preference setting (e.g., autoCancel) */,
  "preference_value" TEXT /* Value of the preference setting (e.g., true/false) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the preference was set */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the preference */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_preferences_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
